steps.title: Identify the contradiction signal
steps.content: The first sentence establishes that many adjectives share origins with their nouns. The second sentence presents an exception: some do not. This opposition between a general pattern and its exception is the key signal.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast/concession — the first sentence states a general pattern (adjectives share origins with their nouns), and the blank introduces an exception to that pattern (some do not).
  • Choice A — ‘Subsequently,’: signals that what follows happens after what came before in time; the exception to the pattern is not a later development — it is a simultaneous counterexample. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘For this reason,’: signals cause-effect, implying the exception is caused by adjectives sharing origins with nouns — which makes no logical sense. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Nevertheless,’: signals that something is true despite what was just established — exactly the structure of a general rule followed by a noted exception. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Specifically,’: signals a narrowing elaboration of the previous point; the exception is not a more specific case of shared origins — it is the opposite. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
